applied_optimizations.name: Triton FP16 matmul kernel with FP32 accumulation
applied_optimizations.description: Implemented Triton matmul kernel using FP16 for tl.dot (required for AMD MFMA instructions) with FP32 accumulation. Key insight: AMD MI355X MFMA requires FP16/BF16 inputs for tl.dot, not FP32. Inputs are cast to FP16 before dot product, accumulator stays in FP32 for precision. Block sizes: 128x128x64 aligned with AMD wavefront size of 64.

attempted_but_failed.name: FP32 Triton matmul
attempted_but_failed.reason: LLVM IR translation error (unrealized_conversion_cast) - tl.dot on ROCm requires FP16/BF16 inputs
attempted_but_failed.error: failed to translate module to LLVM IR

attempted_but_failed.name: Larger block sizes (256x256x64)
attempted_but_failed.reason: Performance regression - increased runtime from 1.08ms to 2.33ms

notes: Key finding: AMD MI355X (gfx950, CDNA4) MFMA instructions require FP16/BF16 inputs for tl.dot
Working pattern: Load FP32 -> cast to FP16 -> tl.dot with FP32 accumulator -> store FP32
Best block configuration: BLOCK_M=128, BLOCK_N=128, BLOCK_K=64 (multiples of 64 for wavefront alignment)
Achieved 1.84x speedup (1.99ms -> 1.08ms) over PyTorch reference matmul
